data.title: Halper Sadeh LLC Encourages SEMR, EXAS, MRSN Shareholders to Contact the Firm to Discuss Their Rights
data.description:
Halper Sadeh LLC Encourages SEMR, EXAS, MRSN Shareholders to Contact the Firm to Discuss Their Rights

Halper Sadeh LLC Encourages SEMR, EXAS, MRSN Shareholders to Contact the Firm to Discuss Their Rights

PR Newswire

NEW YORK, Nov. 28, 2025

Shareholders should contact the firm immediately as there may be limited time to enforce your rights.

NEW YORK, Nov. 28, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Halper Sadeh LLC, an investor rights law firm, is investigating the following companies for potential violations of the federal securities laws and/or breaches of fiduciary duties to shareholders relating to:

Semrush Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: SEMR)'s sale to Adobe for $12.00 per share. If you are a Semrush shareholder, click here to learn more about your rights and options.

Exact Sciences Corporation (NASDAQ: EXAS)'s sale to Abbott for $105.00 per common share in cash. If you are an Exact Sciences shareholder, click here to learn more about your legal rights and options.

Mersana Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ: MRSN)'s sale to Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. If you are a Mersana shareholder, click here to learn more about your legal rights and options.

Halper Sadeh LLC may seek increased consideration for shareholders, additional disclosures and information concerning the proposed transaction, or other relief and benefits on behalf of shareholders. We would handle the action on a contingent fee basis, whereby you would not be responsible for out-of-pocket payment of our legal fees or expenses.

Shareholders are encouraged to contact the firm free of charge to discuss their legal rights and options. Please call Daniel Sadeh or Zachary Halper at (212) 763-0060 or email sadeh@halpersadeh.com or zhalper@halpersadeh.com.

Halper Sadeh LLC represents investors all over the world who have fallen victim to securities fraud and corporate misconduct. Our attorneys have been instrumental in implementing corporate reforms and recovering millions of dollars on behalf of defrauded investors.

Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

data.title: Canadian Economy Rebounds by More Than Expected — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart 




  OTTAWA--Canada's economy recovered far more strongly than anticipated in the latest quarter, pulled out of its decline by a bounce-back in net trade and a surge in defense spending that helped mask weak domestic demand. 



  Gross domestic product grew at an annualized rate of 2.6%, Statistics Canada said Friday. That was the fastest pace since the final three months of last year, and considerably stronger than the modest 0.5% growth forecast by economists and the central bank. 



  The rebound confirms Canada avoided a technical recession, characterized by back-to-back quarterly contractions, after GDP in the second quarter retreated a revised 1.8% when exports slumped in the wake of the U.S. administration's tariffs. However, the economy beneath the surface continues to show signs of struggling and the final quarter of 2025 began on shaky footing. 



  Growth in the third quarter owed much to a sharp drop in imports, the first decline since the final quarter of 2022. Exports nudged higher, which counted as a boost to the economy since the trade balance strengthened. The country's terms of trade--the net income pocketed from the sale of exports--grew 1.0%, the largest increase since the third quarter of 2023, thanks to a lift in export prices, particularly for energy products. 



  Government investment also was up strongly, led by a significant rise in spending on weapons systems as well as investment in institutional buildings such as hospitals. And residential investment increased during the quarter as home resale activity continued to pick up after the weakness seen at the start of 2025. 



  However, there are signs an elevated unemployment rate and the uncertainty that has accompanied President Trump's trade policies continue to be drags on activity. 



  Capital investment by businesses was flat, and the build-up of inventories cooled, with a slowdown in stockpiling by sectors including manufacturing, transportation and utilities. 



  And despite spending more on rent, Canadian household consumption overall edged lower in the third quarter with a fall in outlays on passenger vehicles. Canadians also spent less abroad and made fewer international trips. On a per capita basis, household spending fell 0.2% after the 1.0% rise in the second quarter that helped soften the economy's contraction. 



  Final domestic demand, a gauge of the underlying momentum of the economy that measures spending by all sectors, inched down 0.1% in annual terms for the quarter after expanding 3.5% in the second quarter. 



  "Growth roared back far stronger than anyone expected in the third quarter, but dig deeper and the engine still looks like it's running on fumes," said Royce Mendes, head of macroeconomics at Desjardins. 



  He and other economists pointed to the seeming evaporation of momentum for the economy to begin the fourth quarter, which, despite the surprise headline strength for the third quarter, is likely to keep the Bank of Canada from cutting interest rates further this year. 



  Statistics Canada estimated that industry-level GDP fell 0.3% in October from the month before, which would represent the sharpest decline in monthly activity since the end of 2022. That may in part reflect an Alberta teachers' strike, which more than countered a boost in spending tied to the Toronto Blue Jay's World Series run. 



  Early accounts point to decreases in oil and gas extraction, education services and manufacturing, but increases in mining and in support services. 



  It comes after GDP grew 0.2% in September, more than recouping the revised 0.1% decline the month before, thanks to manufacturing and recovery in air transportation following the end of an Air Canada flight attendants strike that disrupted flights. 



  The Bank of Canada has forecast weak growth through the second half of 2025 that gradually strengthens as businesses adjust to the new trade reality of President Trump's isolationist policies and a struggling labor market that is weighing on household spending. The central bank, which has signaled interest rates may be at about the right level following successive cuts in September and October, has forecast annual growth averaging about 1.4% over 2026 and 2027. 



  "We shouldn't be fooled by the strong headline growth number. Canada's domestic economy remains weak," said Charles St-Arnaud, chief economist at credit union trade association Alberta Central and a former Bank of Canada economist. "With uncertainty remaining high and little indication that the U.S. could remove its tariffs, any improvement in economic activity in the coming quarters is likely to be modest." 





  Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com 


 
 



  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  November 28, 2025 12:41 ET (17:41 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: InventHelp Inventor Develops New Tricep Bar (CHK-3180)
data.description:
InventHelp Inventor Develops New Tricep Bar (CHK-3180)

InventHelp Inventor Develops New Tricep Bar (CHK-3180)

PR Newswire

PITTSBURGH, Nov. 28, 2025

PITTSBURGH, Nov. 28, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- "As a gym owner, I wanted to create a way to target all of the muscles in the triceps," said an inventor, from Tinley Park, Ill., "so I invented THE ULTIMATE TRICEP BAR. My design would allow a strength-training or weightlifting enthusiast to use one strength-training bar for isolating all of the muscles in the triceps."

The patent-pending invention provides a more effective upper arm bar attachment for strength-training enthusiasts. In doing so, it would effectively target all of the tricep muscles. It also increases resistance, and it offers an improved alternative to conventional bar attachments. The invention features a practical design that is easy to use so it is ideal for fitness enthusiasts, gyms, etc. Additionally, a prototype model and technical drawings are available upon request.

The original design was submitted to the Chicago sales office of InventHelp. It is currently available for licensing or sale to manufacturers or marketers. For more information, write Dept. 24-CHK-3180, InventHelp, 100 Beecham Drive, Suite 110, Pittsburgh, PA 15205-9801, or call (412) 288-1300 ext. 1368. Learn more about InventHelp's Invention Submission Services at http://www.InventHelp.com.

data.title: Newly released facts from Social Security demolish Elon Musk's fraud claims - again
data.description:
By Brett Arends

We taxpayers are still waiting for our $1 trillion of annual savings. This latest report shows we will be waiting a long time. Until eternity, actually.

"It's often impossible to prove a negative, but the latest report is yet another one suggesting that this big pot of gold just doesn't exist - and never did," writes Brett Arends.

I'm not surprised that President Donald Trump and the Social Security Administration put out the latest inspector-general report the day before Thanksgiving, when nobody is paying attention.

It's yet another embarrassment.

The latest 57-page report to Congress details a variety of Social Security frauds that took place under Trump's first administration, only to be caught, stopped and prosecuted ... er ... under Joe Biden.

And it confirms what has long been suspected, and which will come as no surprise to MarketWatch readers: namely that Elon Musk and Trump were talking total nonsense for the first six months of this year, when they were claiming that there was a "huge" amount of fraud in Social Security, including hundreds of thousands of dead people claiming benefits.

None of this, of course, will catch up with the Trillion-Dollar Man himself, Musk, who got months of juicy headlines promising huge fraud discoveries, then fled town before the facts arrived. Just before the 2024 presidential election, Musk promised voters he would find "$2 trillion" in savings from the federal budget, though it later turned out he was supposed to say only $1 trillion. Well, the great entrepreneur must have had the budget savings number confused with the Tesla (TSLA) pay deal he was simultaneously in pursuit of.

We taxpayers are still waiting for our $1 trillion of annual savings. And, importantly, we are still waiting for the Social Security program to be rescued from its imminent funding crisis by the exposure of all this waste and fraud.

As the latest report shows, we will be waiting a long time. Until eternity, actually.

The savings are so small that the report actually spells them out in full - "$194,380,293" in fines and restitution, for example, rather than, say, "$194.4 million" or even "$0.194 billion."

Social Security spends $1.6 trillion a year - or, to put it in those same terms, $1,600,000,000,000. It has 70 million beneficiaries, or, if you prefer, 70,000,000.

So we may have to calm our excitement that total fines and restitution in the first half came to $194 million, as mentioned, or that another $287 million in costs have been "questioned," or that another $819 million has been "put to better use."

That last figure represents the total amount of "improper payments" that the inspector general's team reckon the Social Security Administration has avoided making during the past six months.

The figure during the equivalent six months last year, with Biden in the Oval Office? Er ... $10.5 billion.

In total, the inspector general says that if all of the efficiency recommendations were put into effect, the total savings would equal $6.68 billion. That's a total figure, not a recurring, annual one.

The total number of "impostor scam" allegations received in total is 27,665, the report says. This isn't the number of scams, just the number of allegations. This includes people nationwide claiming that a scam artist called them up and falsely said they were from the Social Security Administration, as well as all those allegations of people falsely and fraudulently trying to claim Social Security benefits.

During the six-month period, the Social Security Administration referred 845 people to the U.S. Department of Justice for prosecution for fraud or attempted fraud, and another 111 to state or local prosecutors.

Look, I don't mean to be churlish. It is always good news if they find waste or fraud and they stop it. That should be obvious. And I don't mean to underappreciate the people at the Social Security inspector general's office, who I am sure do fine work. As a taxpayer, a Social Security in-payer, and someone who appreciates efficiency and good government, I am grateful.

But for a period of several months earlier this year, there was a massive political and propaganda campaign run out of Washington, D.C., that sought to persuade us that there was gigantic, epic, "yuge" waste, fraud and abuse in these federal programs.

It's not news to anyone - at least, anyone over about age 14 - that there is some waste, fraud and abuse in a $1.6 trillion program. Your household pet probably could have worked that out. The claim was that it was enormous, that there was a huge conspiracy to hide it, and that when Trump, Musk, Howard Lutnick and the so-called and now-disbanded Department of Government Efficiency got into the bureaucracy it was going to root it out. And, above all, the claim was that the fraud was so vast that it was going to make a material difference to the budget, to taxes and to Social Security. We could slash taxes, even at a time of eye-watering federal deficits, because putting a stop to all the fraud was going to pay for it.

It's famously difficult to prove a negative, but the latest report is yet another one suggesting that this big pot of gold just doesn't exist - and never did.

Which, as I say, helps explain why they put it out the day before Thanksgiving.

-Brett Arends

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  11-28-25 1234ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: ICE Canola Futures Nudging Higher
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Intercontinental Exchange canola futures were higher mid-morning Friday, gleaning support from gains in the Chicago soy complex, Malaysian palm oil and MATIF rapeseed.

Although global crude oil was mixed, increases in West Texas Intermediate were spilling over into the vegetable oils.

With reduced trading hours today in U.S. markets and activity moving into holiday mode, activity could be more volatile.

"The juniors of the juniors are trading today," an analyst said.

As the January canola contract remained above its 20- and 50-day moving averages it was only a few dollars behind its 100-day average.

Canola exports fell back during the week ended Nov. 23 at 99,500 metric tons compared to 284,600 the previous week, the Canadian Grain

Commission reported. Cumulative exports tallied 1.93 million tons versus 3.69 million the same time last year. Year-to-date domestic usage reached 3.69 million tons compared to 3.73 million a year ago.

The canola market will be positioning ahead of Thursday's production report from Statistics Canada. The trade firmly believes the 2025/26 canola harvest is much bigger than the 20.03 million tons the data agency estimated in September.

The Canadian dollar was stronger at mid-session Friday, with the loonie improving to 71.62 U.S. cents, compared to Thursday's close of 71.26.

Approximately 22,150 canola contracts were traded as of 11:32 EST. 




  Prices in Canadian dollars per metric ton: 


Canola      Price     Change 
Jan        653.60    up 2.00 
Mar        666.70    up 2.20 
May        677.10    up 2.70 
Jul        682.30    up 2.80 
 



  Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  November 28, 2025 12:22 ET (17:22 GMT)

data.title: The Week in Oil: Ukraine Peace Negotiations in Focus
data.description:
By Giulia Petroni 




  Here's a look at what happened in oil markets in the week of Nov. 24-28 and what the focus will be in the days to come. 





  OVERVIEW: Oil prices are little changed in evening trade in Europe, with Brent crude at around $63 a barrel and WTI at $59 a barrel after the CME exchange resumed trading following an outage. Investors are monitoring progress in negotiations to end the war in Ukraine as a peace deal could lead to the lifting of U.S. sanctions. 





  MACRO: Dovish comments from some U.S. policymakers earlier this week boosted expectations that the Federal Reserve will lower interest rates again at its next meeting in December. According to the FedWatch tool, traders now price in a nearly 87% chance of a 25-basis-point cut. 





  GEOPOLITICAL RISKS: Renewed hopes for an end to the war in Ukraine have put pressure on oil prices this week. "A ceasefire would likely stop mutual attacks on energy infrastructure, and sanctions might be eased or even lifted," analysts at Commerzbank said. "However, it seems unlikely that the parties will quickly agree on a peace plan." 





  SUPPLY AND DEMAND: The Energy Information Administration's weekly inventory report was bearish for oil. U.S. crude stocks increased by 2.8 million barrels, in stark contrast with the 1.3 million-barrel decline expected by analysts. Gasoline and distillate fuel stocks also increased, pointing to weaker demand in the world's top consumer. 



  Concerns over softer consumption trends and signs of excess supplies continue to weigh on the oil market, with traders expecting prices to fall even further next year. 





  WHAT'S AHEAD: Key members of the OPEC+ alliance are set to meet on Sunday to discuss production policy. Market watchers, though, expect the group to keep policy unchanged and stick to its decision to pause output hikes in the first quarter of next year. 



  "The fundamental outlook remains fairly similar to where it was at the group's last meeting," ING analysts said. "Admittedly, though, there's more uncertainty over Russian oil supply." 



  Coordination on production quotas remains crucial, analysts said, as some countries continue to exceed their targets. According to Commerzbank, Iraq could request a higher production quota due to the resumption of oil deliveries via a pipeline in the north and increased production capacity. 



  Meanwhile, top exporter Saudi Aramco is expected to release official selling prices next week. 



  Looking at the U.S. economic calendar, traders will keep a close eye on PMI data on Monday and ADP employment figures on Wednesday. The focus will then shift to delayed PCE inflation data on Friday. New sentiment indicators from China are also expected. 





  Write to Giulia Petroni at giulia.petroni@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  November 28, 2025 12:22 ET (17:22 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: 'At 55, I am the only single one in the family': Was it wrong to skip Thanksgiving dinner?
data.description:
By Quentin Fottrell

'The same snarky political comments leave me with a bad taste in my mouth. That, and the undercooked turkey.'

"I've finally had enough. Why force myself to go through with this charade again?" (Photo subjects are models.)

Dear Quentin,

Last Thanksgiving was full of backhanded compliments and pointed remarks. Despite my best efforts to steer the conversation away from politics, it did not go well. The same snarky political comments leave me with a bad taste in my mouth. That, and the undercooked turkey. Do I tell my sister and brother-in-law that the turkey was undercooked last year and could have given us all food poisoning?

My narcissistic sister does not talk about anything other than herself, my mother acquiesces to everyone's demands, but seems to condone poor behavior in the process, and my nieces and nephews have never really gotten the time to get to know me. (Nor have I gotten to know them, if I'm being honest, mostly due to my poor relationship with their parents.) I'm invited because, at 55, I am the only single one in the family.

My question is not so much how to navigate difficult conversations - I smile and grit my teeth and have another glass of wine - but more if it's OK to say "no, thank you" to an invite this year. That's what I've done. My mother did her best to cajole me into going. It's not very festive, but I'd rather skip it and do Friendsgiving. Is that selfish of me? I've finally had enough. I'm burned out by the drama.

Why force myself to go through with this charade again?

Single Sister

Related: My liberal sister wants to school me on the 'real causes' of inflation. Trump and politics are off limits for Thanksgiving. What's left to talk about?

You can email The Moneyist with any financial and ethical questions at qfottrell@marketwatch.com. The Moneyist regrets he cannot reply to questions individually.

A lot of time - and money - goes into preparing Thanksgiving dinner, so it's better for everyone to want to be where they want to be.

Dear Single,

Yes, it's OK to say no. You have every right to feel safe, respected and relaxed on Thanksgiving.

You can't change your sister. You also can't change your brother-in-law. You can't change your mom. You can't even change the undercooked turkey - unless you send your sister an anonymous note to preheat the oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit, cover the turkey loosely with tin foil, and roast it for 45 minutes before reducing the heat to 325degF, signing off as "a concerned friend." Yes, tell your sister how to ensure a perfectly cooked turkey. That is a helpful gift; you could even frame it as "round-robin" advice so she doesn't think you are referencing last year's affair.

No one wants to intentionally ruin Thanksgiving by serving an undercooked bird, by serving a cold front to their guests, or by being a difficult or uptight guest. I'm sure your sister was aware the turkey was underdone last year - as was everyone else - and you all did your best to (perhaps unwisely) eat the bits that felt cooked because you did not wish to offend her. It's a lot of stress and pressure to host a Thanksgiving dinner, including for guests. Your feelings are legitimate. It's better to be there as your most authentic, willing self. If not, alternate plans are fine.

You're setting a boundary for yourself. It gives everyone space. It's a gift that keeps on giving all day long.

Your sister may indeed be narcissistic, or it could be a combination of factors where you each rub the other the wrong way. I'm not a psychiatrist, so I'll leave medical diagnoses to medical doctors. But it's OK to have time away from people you feel have hurt you. Keep your side of the street clean. "Mean" comments can sometimes just be ill-judged humor intended to defuse the atmosphere, and, yes, sometimes they can be genuinely mean. You're setting a boundary for yourself. It gives everyone space. It's a gift that keeps on giving all day long.

You can be upset with your family, or you can accept that they will always be who they are. You can love them from afar, or you can love them from the other side of the kitchen table - just not necessarily every year. As long as you are genuinely at peace with your decision, that's OK. If you feel the urge, you can also call and wish everyone a happy Thanksgiving. Small gestures can have a big impact. That said, you have self-assurance and agency, and at 55, it's good to flex those muscles. No one likes to feel like they are the default guest.

Celebrate this Thanksgiving liberation - this holiday renaissance - where you fashion the kind of day you would like to experience with friends who you feel confident won't push your buttons or trigger any childhood patterns. Tell your mom - who I assume is in her late 70s or 80s - that you love her and that you're looking forward to seeing her after Thanksgiving. Like most parents, she probably just wants you to be happy, and she doesn't need or want grief around the holidays. Check in with her on the day; remember, you and your mom may have fewer Thanksgivings left than you think.

Expensive endeavor

A lot of time - and money - goes into preparing Thanksgiving dinner, so it's better for everyone to be where they truly want to be. For the record, the American Farm Bureau Federation's 40th annual Thanksgiving dinner survey says the classic holiday feast for 10 will cost $55.18, or about $5.52 per person, down 5% from last year. "Three years of declines don't fully erase dramatic increases that led to a record-high cost of $64.05 in 2022," it says. No one wants to pay all that money to walk on eggshells, especially given the cost of eggs.

I have a healthy skepticism about Thanksgiving dinner-price surveys, not least because they don't include alcohol and other snacks that guests expect, and that hosts expect guests to bring. Prices also vary by store, city and menu. But let's return to your Friendsgiving. It's not selfish to put yourself first. Be realistic about what you will experience on Thanksgiving with friends, too. There's so much pressure around the holidays to be happy. Friends sometimes want to impress on big occasions, and friends have friends you may not like, and you may find that old irritation returning.

The undercooked turkey may act as a metaphor for your relationship with your sister: it's not quite where it could be.

Sibling relationships don't develop in a straight line. Sometimes they improve. Other times they get worse until they become unbearable. You can sit this one out and see if absence makes the sisters grow fonder of each other. I do agree with you on one thing: Hurtful comments are impossible to take back. Your friends may be glad to have you, and you deserve to feel valued, appreciated, and "seen" on special days such as this. As for your sister, actions and words have consequences.

It's a timely reminder that your family and your friends are just people too. You took a break from your family this year. You may wish to take a break from your friends next year. Hopefully that won't happen, but nothing is set in stone. You are a free agent and have the flexibility to spend the holidays how you wish. This does not have to be an opening salvo in a cold war with your family or a coded message to your sister. You're merely flexible with your plans.

The undercooked turkey may act as a metaphor for your relationship with your sister. It's not quite where it could be, and it leaves you feeling wanting. Only you know whether sitting down and talking about how her behavior makes you feel might lead to a mutually agreeable breakthrough. It's good if it does, and it's good if it doesn't. We don't have to be friends with everyone. And if the turkey is undercooked this year? Say something early to avoid potential salmonella poisoning.

As with last year's chicken, your sister's apparent inability to gauge the temperature in the room is the real root of the problem.

Related: My sons will each inherit $500K laundromats from their grandparents. How do we keep their spouses out of it?

By emailing your questions to the Moneyist or posting your dilemmas on the Moneyist Facebook group, you agree to have them published anonymously on MarketWatch.

Previous columns by Quentin Fottrell:

There's a lot of tension': My brother and I manage a $5 million trust. How do we solve our many disagreements?

Beware of this Black Friday scam

My sons will each inherit $500K laundromats from their grandparents. How do we keep their spouses out of it?

Check out the Moneyist private Facebook group, where members help answer life's thorniest money issues. Post your questions, or weigh in on the latest Moneyist columns.

By submitting your story to Dow Jones & Co., the publisher of MarketWatch, you understand and agree that we may use your story, or versions of it, in all media and platforms, including via third parties.

-Quentin Fottrell

  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  11-28-25 1207ET

data.title: STUB INVESTOR ALERT: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Announces that StubHub Holdings, Inc. Investors with Substantial Losses Have Opportunity to Lead Class Action Lawsuit
data.description:
STUB INVESTOR ALERT: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Announces that StubHub Holdings, Inc. Investors with Substantial Losses Have Opportunity to Lead Class Action Lawsuit

STUB INVESTOR ALERT: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Announces that StubHub Holdings, Inc. Investors with Substantial Losses Have Opportunity to Lead Class Action Lawsuit

PR Newswire

NEW YORK, Nov. 28, 2025

NEW YORK, Nov. 28, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Attorney Advertising -- Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC, a nationally recognized law firm, notifies investors that a class action lawsuit has been filed against StubHub Holdings, Inc. ("StubHub" or "the Company") (NYSE: STUB) and certain of its officers.

Class Definition

This lawsuit seeks to recover damages against Defendants for alleged violations of the federal securities laws on behalf of all persons and entities that purchased or otherwise acquired StubHub securities pursuant to the registration statement and prospectus issued in connection with the Company's September 17, 2025 initial public offering ("IPO"). Such investors are encouraged to join this case by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/STUB.

Case Details

This Complaint alleges that the Registration Statement was materially false and misleading and omitted to state: (1) the Company was experiencing changes in the timing of payments to vendors; (2) those changes had a significant adverse impact on free cash flow, including trailing 12 months ("TTM") free cash flow; (3) as a result, the Company's free cash flow reports were materially misleading; and (4) that, as a result of the foregoing, Defendants' positive statements about the Company's business, operations, and prospects, were materially misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis.

What's Next?

A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to review a copy of the Complaint, you can visit the firm's site: bgandg.com/STUB. or you may contact Peretz Bronstein, Esq. or his Client Relations Manager, Nathan Miller, of Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC at 332-239-2660. If you suffered a loss in StubHub you have until January 23, 2026, to request that the Court appoint you as lead plaintiff. Your ability to share in any recovery doesn't require that you serve as lead plaintiff.

There is No Cost to You

We represent investors in class actions on a contingency fee basis. That means we will ask the court to reimburse us for out-of-pocket expenses and attorneys' fees, usually a percentage of the total recovery, only if we are successful.

Why Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman

Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is a nationally recognized firm that represents investors in securities fraud class actions and shareholder derivative suits. Our firm has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors nationwide.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn, X, Facebook, or Instagram.

Attorney advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

Contact

Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC
Peretz Bronstein or Nathan Miller
332-239-2660 | info@bgandg.com

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/stub-investor-alert-bronstein-gewirtz--grossman-llc-announces-that-stubhub-holdings-inc-investors-with-substantial-losses-have-opportunity-to-lead-class-action-lawsuit-302626081.html

SOURCE Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC

data.title: VRNS Investigation Alert:  Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP Encourages Varonis Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ: VRNS) Investors with Significant Losses to Contact the Firm
data.description:
VRNS Investigation Alert: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP Encourages Varonis Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ: VRNS) Investors with Significant Losses to Contact the Firm

VRNS Investigation Alert: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP Encourages Varonis Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ: VRNS) Investors with Significant Losses to Contact the Firm

PR Newswire

RADNOR, Pa., Nov. 28, 2025

RADNOR, Pa., Nov. 28, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- The law firm of Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP (www.ktmc.com) is currently investigating potential violations of the federal securities laws on behalf of investors of Varonis Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ: VRNS) ("Varonis").

On October 28, 2025, Varonis reported its financial results for the third quarter of 2025 and revealed revenue which missed consensus estimates, including a 63.9% decline in term license subscription revenues, year over year. Varonis also disclosed it was "reducing our full-year ARR guidance to account for the underperformance of [its] on-prem subscription business." Addressing the poor results, Varonis stated that the company's on-premises subscription business is a "drag on total company ARR growth," citing a number of factors which contributed to "lower renewal rate of on-prem subscription[s]," including "sales process issues."

On this news, Varonis' stock price fell $30.66 per share, or 48.67%, to close at $32.34 per share on October 29, 2025.

If you are a Varonis investor and would like to learn more about our investigation, please CLICK HERE to fill out our online form or contact Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP:  Jonathan Naji, Esq. (484) 270-1453 or E-mail at info@ktmc.com. You can also click on the following link or paste it in your browser:  https://www.ktmc.com/varonis-systems-inc-investigation?utm_source=PR_Newswire&mktm=PR    

Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP prosecutes class actions in state and federal courts throughout the country involving securities fraud, breaches of fiduciary duties and other violations of state and federal law. Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP is a driving force behind corporate governance reform, and has recovered billions of dollars on behalf of institutional and individual investors from the United States and around the world.  The firm represents investors, consumers and whistleblowers (private citizens who report fraudulent practices against the government and share in the recovery of government dollars).  For more information about Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP, please visit www.ktmc.com.  

CONTACT:

Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP
Jonathan Naji, Esq.
280 King of Prussia Road
Radnor, PA 19087
(484) 270-1453
info@ktmc.com

May be considered attorney advertising in certain jurisdictions.  Past results do not guarantee future outcomes.

data.title: ARE Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Alexandria Real Estate Equities, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit
data.description:
ARE Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Alexandria Real Estate Equities, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit

ARE Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Alexandria Real Estate Equities, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit

PR Newswire

NEW YORK, Nov. 28, 2025

NEW YORK, Nov. 28, 2025 /PRNewswire/ --

Why: Rosen Law Firm, a global investor rights law firm, announces a class action lawsuit on behalf of purchasers of securities of Alexandria Real Estate Equities, Inc. (NYSE: ARE) between January 27, 2025 and October 27, 2025, both dates inclusive (the "Class Period"). A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to serve as lead plaintiff, you must move the Court no later than January 26, 2026.

So what: If you purchased Alexandria Real Estate Equities securities during the Class Period you may be entitled to compensation without payment of any out of pocket fees or costs through a contingency fee arrangement.

What to do next: To join the Alexandria Real Estate Equities class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=48531 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action. A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to serve as lead plaintiff, you must move the Court no later than January 26, 2026. A lead plaintiff is a representative party acting on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation.

Why Rosen Law: We encourage investors to select qualified counsel with a track record of success in leadership roles. Often, firms issuing notices do not have comparable experience, resources, or any meaningful peer recognition. Be wise in selecting counsel. The Rosen Law Firm represents investors throughout the globe, concentrating its practice in securities class actions and shareholder derivative litigation. Rosen Law Firm achieved, at that time, the largest ever securities class action settlement against a Chinese Company. Rosen Law Firm was Ranked No. 1 by ISS Securities Class Action Services for number of securities class action settlements in 2017. The firm has been ranked in the top 4 each year since 2013 and has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors. In 2019 alone the firm secured over $438 million for investors. In 2020, founding partner Laurence Rosen was named by law360 as a Titan of Plaintiffs' Bar. Many of the firm's attorneys have been recognized by Lawdragon and Super Lawyers.

Details of the case: According to the lawsuit, defendants provided investors with material information concerning Alexandria Real Estate's expected revenue and funds from operations ("FFO") growth for the 2025 fiscal year, particularly as it related to the growth of Alexandria Real Estate's real estate operations. The defendants' statements included, among other things, confidence in Alexandria Real Estate Equities' lease activity, occupancy stability, and ability to develop its tenant pipeline.

According to the lawsuit, defendants provided these overwhelmingly positive statements to investors while, at the same time, disseminating materially false and misleading statements and/or concealing material adverse facts concerning the true state of its Long Island City ("LIC") property. In particular, Alexandria Real Estate's claims and confidence about the leasing value of the LIC property as a life-science destination. When the true details entered the market, the lawsuit claims that investors suffered damages.

To join the Alexandria Real Estate Equities class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=48531 call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action.

No Class Has Been Certified. Until a class is certified, you are not represented by counsel unless you retain one. You may select counsel of your choice. You may also remain an absent class member and do nothing at this point. An investor's ability to share in any potential future recovery is not dependent upon serving as lead plaintiff.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-rosen-law-firm, on Twitter: https://twitter.com/rosen_firm or on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/rosenlawfirm/.

Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

Contact Information:

New York, NY 10016
